title: eriogonum microtheca var. effusum
intro: Eriogonum microtheca var. effusum, also known as Sulphur Flower Buckwheat, is a perennial plant species in the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The Sulphur Flower Buckwheat has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically to the skin or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
